key_points: The theorem helps determine unknown sides of right triangles
The diagonal of rectangles and squares can be derived using the theorem
Many geometric constructions rely on right triangle relationships
Pythagorean triples can be scaled to create larger right triangles
The theorem is widely used in geometry, engineering, and construction
examples: Ladder problem: ladder 13 m long placed 5 m from wall reaches height 12 m
Rectangle with sides 20 and 21 has diagonal 29
exam_tips: Use the theorem to verify if a triangle is right-angled
Apply algebra carefully when finding missing sides
Draw a diagram for real-life problems like ladders and heights
